hypothesis: Reducing the onboarding checklist from 8 steps to 4 steps will increase completion rate by at least 10 percentage points.
result: Onboarding completion rate increased from 54% to 63% in the treatment group over 14 days.
interpretation: Reducing cognitive load at signup has a measurable positive effect. The removed steps (team invite, integration setup, notification preferences) can be deferred to in-product prompts without hurting activation.

hypothesis: Offering three pre-configured export column presets will reduce support tickets related to reporting by 20%.
result: Support ticket volume for reporting dropped 11% over 3 weeks. Not statistically significant at current traffic.
interpretation: Directionally positive but inconclusive. The preset approach may be right but the specific presets chosen do not match what users actually need. Requires follow-up qualitative research to identify the right default columns.

hypothesis: Showing recent and related project suggestions when the search bar is focused will reduce failed searches.
result: No significant change in failed search rate. Click-through on suggestions was under 4%.
interpretation: Users do not want suggestions — they want better result matching. The problem is query matching quality, not discoverability. Suggestions may add noise rather than value. Consider improving fuzzy match logic instead.
